name: Peach (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (concentration_mM assumes ~0.1 L water per 100 g flesh, ~89% water)

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Dominant sugar in peach flesh; USDA NLEA sugar panel. Corroborated by Zhang 2022 (sucrose 42-94 mg/g, 60-86% of total sugar).

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: USDA individual-sugar panel; glucose slightly exceeds fructose in peach, unlike apple.

components.name: D-sorbitol
components.source: Zhang et al. 2022, Foods PMC9563965 (1.19-3.72 mg/g FW, midpoint ~2.5)
components.evidence: Rosaceae translocated sugar alcohol; fermentable by many microbes. Not in USDA panel.

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Zhang et al. 2022, Foods PMC9563965 (3.40-7.73 mg/g FW, 32-59% of total organic acid; midpoint ~5.6 mg/g)
components.evidence: DOMINANT organic acid of peach; a major non-sugar carbon/energy source omitted by the FooDB profile.

components.name: succinic acid
components.source: Zhang et al. 2022, Foods PMC9563965 (0.43-4.13 mg/g FW; midpoint ~2.3 mg/g)
components.evidence: Third organic acid (malic:quinic:succinic ~4:2:1 = >80% of total acids). High cultivar variability.

components.name: quinic acid
components.source: Zhang et al. 2022, Foods PMC9563965 (0.97-2.81 mg/g FW, 15-29% of total; midpoint ~1.9 mg/g)
components.evidence: Second organic acid; shikimate-pathway intermediate, growth-relevant.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Zhang et al. 2022, Foods PMC9563965 (0.34-1.75 mg/g FW; midpoint ~1.0 mg/g)
components.evidence: Least abundant of the four major peach organic acids.

components.name: L-aspartate (+ asparagine)
components.source: USDA FDC 169928 (Asp 0.418 g); free-AA profile Wang 2022 PMC9222846
components.evidence: By far the largest amino-acid pool; acid hydrolysis reports asparagine (the dominant free AA in peach, >50% of free AA) as aspartate. Major reduced-N source.

components.name: L-glutamate (+ glutamine)
components.evidence: Second amino-acid pool; free Gln/Glu notable.

components.name: L-methionine
components.evidence: USDA amino-acid panel; likely S-limiting for growth.

components.name: retinol equivalent (vitamin A RAE)
components.evidence: Provitamin-A activity from carotenoids; no preformed retinol in fruit.

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.evidence: Present but not quantified in USDA; retained with null amount.

components.name: phosphate (as P)
components.evidence: USDA mineral panel; phosphorus reported as P.

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Dominant fatty acid of the small peach lipid pool.

components.name: dietary fiber (pectin/cellulose)
components.evidence: Largely pectin; not directly fermentable by most single strains but defines matrix.

remove.name: Silver
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: toxic contaminant, not a nutrient; FooDB environmental noise

remove.name: Cadmium
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a nutrient

remove.name: Lead
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a nutrient

remove.name: Mercury
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a nutrient

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace metal, not a defined peach nutrient

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: cobalt trace not a peach nutrient (no B12 in fruit); FooDB noise

remove.name: Arachidonic acid
remove.reason: 20:4 n-6 is an animal fatty acid, biologically implausible in peach flesh; FooDB predicted-not-measured

remove.name: Aluminum
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant element

remove.name: Strontium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant element

remove.name: Titanium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant element

remove.name: Barium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant element

remove.name: Tin
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant element

remove.name: Zirconium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant element

remove.name: Lithium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant element

remove.name: Silicon
remove.reason: non-nutritive structural trace, not a defined growth nutrient

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: microbially-produced food preservative, implausible endogenous peach compound; FooDB noise

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: furanocoumarin characteristic of citrus/Apiaceae, implausible in peach; FooDB predicted

remove.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: predicted non-physiological stereoisomer; redundant with L-isoleucine

remove.name: (E)-2-Phenyl-2-butenal
remove.reason: trace aroma volatile, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Chinese tannin
remove.reason: ill-defined tannin entry, not a discrete quantifiable growth nutrient

missing_added: full USDA amino-acid, vitamin, mineral and fatty-acid quantities
keep_uncovered: Amygdalin (genuine cyanogenic glycoside, trace in flesh; no BiGG met)
Boron (genuine plant micronutrient; not a microbial growth met)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 169928 (Peaches, raw, yellow) - full macro/sugar/amino-acid/vitamin/mineral/fatty-acid panel

Zhang et al. 2022, Foods, 'Physicochemical Characteristics, Antioxidant Activities, and Aroma Compound Analysis of Seven Peach Cultivars', PMC9563965 - individual sugars, sorbitol, and organic acids (malic/quinic/succinic/citric) by HPLC
Wang et al. 2022, 'Amino Acid Profiles in Peach (Prunus persica L.) Fruit', PMC9222846 - free amino-acid profile (asparagine dominant)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources: sucrose (~4.8 g/100g) plus glucose and fructose, with malic acid (~0.56 g/100g) the dominant organic acid and a major secondary substrate; succinic + quinic + citric complete the acid pool. The FooDB profile omitted ALL organic acids and sorbitol and lacked sugar/nutrient quantities - these are the key additions. Aspartate value (0.418 g) is inflated by asparagine, the dominant free amino acid and main reduced-N source. Organic-acid amounts are cultivar-variable (succinic especially); midpoints of cited HPLC ranges used.
